meaning: n.招展,招摇,炫耀,飘扬v.挥动,夸耀,炫耀,飘扬
examples: It's not an externally observable characteristic unless you want to flaunt it.
Store owners will tell you horror stories about shoppers with attitude, who walk in demanding discounts and flaunt their new power at every turn.
If you don't want it, flaunt it.
These days, at least to corporations, all Delaware does is flaunt itself as a tax haven.
President Obama and his regulatory legions flaunt very heavy, very visible hands pressing partisan self-interest.
